title: Zephyranthes goiana
taxonomy: Zephyranthes goiana is classified in the kingdom Plantae, specifically in the family Amaryllidaceae and the genus Zephyranthes. This genus belongs to the family Amaryllidaceae, which consists of flowering plants commonly known as the amaryllis family. The specific classification of Zephyranthes goiana also includes the order Asparagales, which is a large and diverse order of flowering plants. Within this order, Zephyranthes goiana falls under the family Amaryllidaceae due to its unique characteristics and growth habits. The exact classification of Zephyranthes goiana reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared traits with other plants in the Amaryllidaceae family.

Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Zephyranthes goiana for medicinal purposes.

intro: Zephyranthes goiana, commonly known as the Brazilian rain lily, is a perennial bulbous plant species belonging to the Amaryllidaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Z. goiana include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which have been isolated and studied for their medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Z. goiana is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture to treat various health conditions, and is sometimes used in combination with other plants to enhance its therapeutic effects.
